candidates.problem: Several add-ons independently parse URL, route, thread, and page context.
candidates.payloadBounds: No payload beyond the current route context; caller supplies no selector or URL source.
candidates.resultBounds: Normalized hostname/pathname, page scopes, route generation, and bounded thread ID/title/image fields; no DOM objects.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: No retained resources; result becomes stale when the supplied route generation changes.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core descriptor/wrapper approximately +1.5-2.5 KB readable; consumers should remove duplicated parsers, exact delta requires implementation measurement.
candidates.compatibility: Additive action; older cores require an add-on-local parser fallback.
candidates.reason: Three production add-ons have overlapping route parsing, and a bounded normalized result reduces correctness drift without exposing core DOM.

candidates.problem: Core-page add-ons use selector polling, mount retries, and loading checks with separate timer cleanup.
candidates.scopePolicy: runtime; one-shot, owner-scoped, core page scopes only
candidates.payloadBounds: One allowlisted selector or fixed observer target, required timeout <= 30 seconds, no arbitrary callback.
candidates.resultBounds: At most one sanitized node descriptor or a timeout/cancel result; no unbounded node collection.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Core owns the observer/timer under the add-on owner and cancels it on disable, route invalidation, or teardown.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core observer descriptor approximately +1.5-3 KB readable; removes repeated timer scaffolding only after consumer migration.
candidates.compatibility: Additive action; hybrid add-ons retain a local implementation on standalone hosts.
candidates.reason: At least four add-ons show bounded wait/poll behavior; central ownership directly reduces stale callbacks and cleanup risk.

candidates.problem: Open dialogs are updated through direct content-element lookup or close/reopen loops.
candidates.payloadBounds: Existing dialog ID plus bounded sanitized HTML or structured progress fields; same HTML ceiling as dialog.open.
candidates.resultBounds: { ok, value: { dialogId, updated } } or ownership/size validation error; no DOM reference.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Core updates only the owning dialog and rejects updates after close, disable, or teardown.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core descriptor/wrapper approximately +0.8-1.8 KB readable; consumer savings depend on removing direct content lookup.
candidates.compatibility: Additive action; older cores fall back to existing render or close/reopen behavior.
candidates.reason: Example, Library, and Latest Filters each maintain open dialog content outside the current dialog action.

candidates.problem: Some mounted UI uses separate global click routing instead of declarative core-managed actions.
candidates.scopePolicy: runtime; bounded declared action IDs only
candidates.payloadBounds: Action ID from a manifest/runtime declaration and bounded primitive payload; no selectors or inline code.
candidates.resultBounds: No arbitrary callback; core emits an add-on-scoped command with the declared action ID.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Unmount removes routing automatically; listener ownership belongs to the mount owner.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core event-routing code likely +2-4 KB readable before consumer savings.
candidates.compatibility: Would require a new capability negotiation path and fallback for older cores.
candidates.reason: Only Library currently demonstrates the specific cross-boundary workaround; Example and Latest keep their click handlers inside add-on-owned UI, so the multi-consumer threshold is not met.

callSites.text: export const mountUi = (core, payload) => core.invokeCoreAction("ui.mount", payload);

candidates.problem: Storage refresh and cross-tab behavior could be centralized if multiple add-ons shared the same semantic store.
candidates.scopePolicy: runtime; own add-on storage bucket only
candidates.payloadBounds: One bounded key and sanitized value; no complete core-config payloads or cross-add-on keys.
candidates.resultBounds: Changed key/value metadata only, with local-write loop prevention.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Explicit unsubscribe and owner teardown; listeners must not survive disable.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core listener/reconciliation path likely +2-4 KB readable plus lifecycle tests.
candidates.compatibility: Would require capability negotiation and a separate contract from removed core config synchronization.
candidates.reason: The only value-change listener is Masked + Direct add-on transport, which must remain add-on-owned; Latest Filters uses direct GM only for its local preset fallback. No shared core-storage subscription gap is proven.

candidates.problem: Core-connected add-ons request access and throttle metadata through separate calls or assemble local context.
candidates.scopePolicy: management; read-only, never handshake or secret data
candidates.payloadBounds: No caller payload; response fields must be individually bounded and redacted.
candidates.resultBounds: Existing access and throttle projections plus optional protocol/scope metadata; no config object.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core descriptor/wrapper approximately +1-2 KB readable; may remove one round trip per bootstrap.
candidates.compatibility: Additive action; fallback to addon.access and addon.throttle.
candidates.reason: All core add-ons already use the two stable, separately authorized projections; combining them changes no demonstrated correctness failure and risks mixing management policy with throttling.

candidates.problem: Long imports maintain progress state and dialog update/close behavior in add-on code.
candidates.payloadBounds: Bounded counters, status enum, and message lengths; no arbitrary HTML if structured progress is sufficient.
candidates.resultBounds: Progress update acknowledgement and cancellation state.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Cancel/close aborts the owning operation and removes the progress dialog on teardown.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Core progress family likely +3-6 KB readable; overlaps ui.dialog.update.
candidates.compatibility: New action family would require fallback and duplicate semantics with current dialogs.
candidates.reason: Example and Library are two consumers, but their immediate shared gap is dialog update ownership; measure ui.dialog.update first before adding a larger specialized progress API.

candidates.problem: Add-ons repeat retry loops, timer cleanup, cancellation flags, and stale-commit guards.
candidates.scopePolicy: local add-on runtime; no core action
candidates.payloadBounds: Owner ID, generation, AbortSignal, bounded delay/retry count, and caller-supplied operation.
candidates.resultBounds: Promise result or normalized cancellation; no bridge payload changes.
candidates.ownershipCleanup: Shared helper owns timers and pending operations for one add-on and releases them on disable/teardown.
candidates.estimatedSourceDelta: Shared helper approximately +1-2 KB authored; expected net reduction after at least three consumers migrate.
candidates.compatibility: Source-only refactor; bridge protocol and public actions unchanged.
candidates.reason: This is repeated add-on boilerplate rather than a missing core capability; keep ownership local and avoid expanding the core service.

rejectedOrDeferred.reason: Only Library currently demonstrates the specific cross-boundary workaround; Example and Latest keep their click handlers inside add-on-owned UI, so the multi-consumer threshold is not met.

The only value-change listener is Masked + Direct add-on transport, which must remain add-on-owned; Latest Filters uses direct GM only for its local preset fallback. No shared core-storage subscription gap is proven.
All core add-ons already use the two stable, separately authorized projections; combining them changes no demonstrated correctness failure and risks mixing management policy with throttling.
Example and Library are two consumers, but their immediate shared gap is dialog update ownership; measure ui.dialog.update first before adding a larger specialized progress API.
